title: Headway Raw Record Envelope v0
description: The envelope every connector wraps a raw record in before producing to Kafka (ADR-0006). record_id is the lowercase hex SHA-256 of the exact raw payload bytes (content-addressed, ADR-0007). Raw records are immutable after landing.

envelope_version.description: Envelope contract version. Consumers MUST reject unknown versions.

record_id.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the exact raw payload bytes as received. Content-addressed identity; anchors the lineage graph.

source.description: Source system type, e.g. gtfs_static, gtfs_rt, tides, apc, farebox, j1939, obd2, ev_charging, paratransit, maintenance, manual.

connector_version.description: Semantic version of the producing connector.

agency_id.description: Optional agency identifier for multi-feed deployments. In database-per-agency hosted mode (ADR-0004) the pipeline is already agency-scoped; this field disambiguates multi-agency feeds only.

feed_url.description: Optional URL or locator the payload was fetched from.

fetched_at.description: UTC instant the connector fetched/received the payload.

content_type.description: MIME type of the raw payload, e.g. application/x-protobuf, application/zip, application/json.

payload_encoding.description: base64: payload holds the raw bytes base64-encoded (small records). object_ref: payload holds an object-store key (large records, e.g. GTFS static zips); the object's bytes hash to record_id.

payload.description: Raw payload bytes base64-encoded, or the object-store key, per payload_encoding.

parse_status.description: ok: payload parsed against its source spec at the connector. malformed: it did not — the record is still landed (never dropped) and a data-quality issue MUST be raised downstream (fail loudly).

parse_error.description: Human-readable parse failure detail. Required in practice when parse_status is malformed.
